behavior_summary: When this book object is activated/read, the script sets an in-game info flag to mark discovery/progression and presents a long explanatory book text to the player.
The displayed text directs the player toward the Slayer and mentions a cousin who can provide the Slayer's location.

After constructing the text, the script calls the engine Book::read(book_ptr, string_ptr, pid) to show the UI, frees the constructed string, inspects Book::read's return value (via an implies-style check), then suspends/returns.
notable_control_flow: Entry on book-read activation of the book object.
Immediate write of an info/quest state (via 'set info', inferred id 1) to mark this book as seen/processed.
Dynamic construction of a long text buffer which is passed to Book::read along with the book object pointer and the player id (pid).
Post-call cleanup of the constructed string and an inspection of the Book::read return (used in an implies/pop sequence) before suspending.
open_questions: The exact game-level meaning of the info id written (inferred as id 1) — which quest/flag it maps to in higher-level design is not explicit.
Which subsystem/function actually frees the constructed string buffer (the disassembly shows a free step but the concrete runtime function is not named in the canonical facts).
Whether the Book::read return value influences additional logic elsewhere (only an implies/pop observed here; no further branching in this script).
Which player/process id (pid) value is supplied in all contexts (single-player vs. multiplayer semantics) — only a pid parameter is observed.

main_function.name: U8P_BRIBOOK4::0080
main_function.purpose: Handle the book 'read' activation: mark an in-game info/quest state and present the book's full text to the invoking player via Book::read, then perform cleanup and suspend.
entry_conditions: Activated when the book object is read/activated by the player (book-read event).
Called with a book pointer/handle (book_ptr) and a pid identifying the viewing player/process.

function_map.name: U8P_BRIBOOK4::0080
function_map.role: Book read handler that marks an info flag and displays a long explanatory text via Book::read.

external_calls.purpose: Engine/script opcode used to write an info/quest flag (inferred id 1) on the object to record that the book has been seen or processed.
Open the book-reading UI and display the dynamically constructed book text to the player; parameters: book_ptr, pointer-to-text, pid. Return value is observed and used in an implies/pop sequence.
Engine operation that suspends the script/process after handling the read and cleanup.
A runtime free/cleanup of the dynamically constructed text buffer after Book::read returns (callee not explicitly named in canonical facts; inferred cleanup behavior).

writes.name: info flag (id 1)
writes.likely_effect: Marks that the player has read/seen this book or progressed the related Slayer information; prevents redundant discovery or drives quest/dialog state.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When this book object is activated/read, the script sets an in-game info flag to mark discovery/progression and presents a long explanatory book text to the player.
